item.title: Issue: Roslyn analyzer on Azure Devops? - @sonarjo
item.description:
I am considering to write a Roslyn analyzer to squeeze out bad practices I encountered in our application. Afaik there are two options to distribute Roslyn analyzers:

  • via Nuget - which requires consuming projects to be updated
  • via VSIX - which requires developers to install the option

I.e. both variants require developers to do something. What I am missing is how to install analyzers onto Azure Devops (without updating all projects) in order to enforce analzers to be executed. Is there a means to do that? Thanks, Joachim

item.title: Issue: Stop jagged arrays promotion. Increase complexity max. - @vsfeedback
item.description:
This issue has been moved from a ticket on Developer Community.

[regression] [worked-in:15.1] Stop jagged arrays promotion. My projects are more than 10 years old, The jagged array promotion gives me too many warnings Perhaps suppression of CA1815 & CA1813 &

Original Comments

null on 4/27/2021, 11:18 PM:

Thank you for your feedback! For us to investigate this further, could you please provide a sample solution that has this problem so that we can conduct further research? It would be very helpful if you could provide some screenshots of this problem.

We look forward to hearing from you!

Feedback Bot on 4/27/2021, 11:24 PM:

Thank you for taking the time to provide your suggestion. We will do some preliminary checks to make sure we can proceed further. We'll provide an update once the issue has been triaged by the product team.

Joe on 4/29/2021, 08:55 PM:

It would be good that 50 or more Warnings were not produced every day that I do VB enhancements.
TIA
Ojars Puce

null on 4/29/2021, 11:08 PM:

Thank you for your feedback! In order for us to investigate this further, could you please provide repro steps, a more detailed description of the faulty behavior and the actions you were taking when the issue appeared to help us better understand this scenario? What is the actual result and which result do you expect to happen?

Looking forward to your reply so we can have the necessary information to move this item forward.

Joe on 4/30/2021, 05:16 PM:

**Severity Code Description Project File Line Suppression State

Warning CA1814 ‘NewPicks.CreateArrays()’ uses a multidimensional array of ‘Object(,)’. Replace it with a jagged array if possible. AllNew5 D:\2021 VB\AllNew5A\AllNew5\NewPicks.vb 448 Active

**

Joe on 4/30/2021, 05:18 PM:

at StreamJsonRpc.JsonRpc.d__1391.MoveNext() --- End of stack trace from previous location where exception was thrown --- at System.Runtime.CompilerServices.TaskAwaiter.ThrowForNonSuccess(Task task) at System.Runtime.CompilerServices.TaskAwaiter.HandleNonSuccessAndDebuggerNotification(Task task) at System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask1.get_Result()

null on 5/5/2021, 06:38 PM:

Thank you for taking the time to provide your suggestion. We will do some preliminary checks to make sure we can proceed further. We’ll provide an update once the issue has been triaged by the product team.

item.title: Issue: Anaylzers constantly showing in warning state - @vsfeedback
item.description:
This issue has been moved from a ticket on Developer Community.

I'd like to submit a ticket regarding code analysis. It's a wonderful feature when it works but the stability of it is very questionable. More often than naught, when I open my solution, all the analyzers attempt to pull from a local path that VS complains about despite numerous efforts to pull the same analyzer from NuGet. For a team where their definition of done states "all errors, warnings and information" messages are cleared from the code before checking in, these errors make that exceedlingly difficult. We almost always have "null reference error" on a warning we cannot clear or resolve. I think it would be great, even in an errored state, if we could right click on the 'local' analyzer and choose 'find nuget package' so the redudant activity of trying to find all these packages is reduced.

Original Comments

Feedback Bot on 2/13/2020, 00:01 AM:

We have directed your feedback to the appropriate engineering team for further evaluation. The team will review the feedback and notify you about the next steps.

Feedback Bot on 3/19/2020, 07:32 PM:

I have detected that for the last 35 days, this issue didn't have much product team activity and a very small amount of new votes or comments. Based on this, its severity, and affected area, it’s my experience that this issue is very unlikely to be fixed.

Feedback Bot on 3/31/2020, 11:50 PM:

Thank you for sharing your feedback! Our teams prioritize action on product issues with broad customer impact. See details at: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/visualstudio/ide/report-a-problem?view=vs-2019#faq. In case you need answers to common questions or need assisted support, be sure to use https://visualstudio.microsoft.com/vs/support/. We'll keep you posted on any updates to this feedback.

Feedback Bot on 4/14/2021, 00:32 AM:

Thank you for taking the time to provide your suggestion. We will do some preliminary checks to make sure we can proceed further. We'll provide an update once the issue has been triaged by the product team.

item.title: Issue: Build Optimization Analyzer for long build times - @vsfeedback
item.description:
This issue has been moved from a ticket on Developer Community.

Our solution is fairly complex, and in building it we often find Visual Studio 2019 just seems to hit one csproj at random that it slows to a crawl to build.
Possibly we're doing something sub-optimal, or have a dodgy build.props stage or vs-extension or code-analysis NuGet installed. A verbose-level build output might not surface certain problems, and certain others it may surface but rely on human-inference to connect the dots (e.g. too many 'copy-always' instead of 'preserve-newest') ... if they'd even question it (does component C really need all the localized resources of component A copied to it bin dir too?).

A "build optimization analyzer" feature might be handy to tell what's taking so long and what to do about it.
A related capability might detect why running a unit-test launched a build though a build had just finished. If this is our mistake, or from an optional extension, we'd save a lot of time avoiding the cause.

This could also be used by VS telemetry itself: Roslyn reporting there's 10_000 lines of C# in the component, yet it takes 5 min to build on a 12core box, Msft would probably like to have more visibility into such occurrences in the wild, not just rare, vague, and anecdotal feedback.

Original Comments

Feedback Bot on 7/7/2021, 07:27 PM:

Thank you for taking the time to provide your suggestion. We will do some preliminary checks to make sure we can proceed further. We'll provide an update once the issue has been triaged by the product team.

item.title: Issue: Quick Actions on non-CLS-compliant classes, methods or properties should suggest setting [CLSCompliant(false)] - @vsfeedback
item.description:
This issue has been moved from a ticket on Developer Community.

I've recently encountered Warning BC40026: "Type is not CLS-compliant because it derives from base type that is not CLS-compliant" as well as BC40028: "Type of parameter is not CLS-compliant". There isn't much I could do about it, since those mentiond base class and type are from a Microsoft dll. So, naturally, I went to Quick-Actions on offending code to fix the warnings. But the only option I had was to suppress the warning.

I'm pretty sure the actually clean way to deal with those warnings is to set CLSCompliant(false)-Attribute.

Original Comments

Feedback Bot on 7/4/2021, 07:36 PM:

Thank you for taking the time to provide your suggestion. We will do some preliminary checks to make sure we can proceed further. We'll provide an update once the issue has been triaged by the product team.

item.title: Issue: False positive CA1508, condition is always true - @myblindy
item.description:
Analyzer

Diagnostic ID: CA1508: 'encodedBytes is null' is always 'true'. Remove or refactor the condition(s) to avoid dead code.

Analyzer source

SDK: Built-in CA analyzers in .NET 6 SDK

Version: SDK 6.0.100-preview.6.21355.2

Describe the bug

The analyzer seems confused by async tasks:

using System;
using System.Threading.Tasks;

byte[] encodedBytes = default;
await Task.Run(() => encodedBytes = new byte[1]).ConfigureAwait(false);
if (encodedBytes is null)
    Console.WriteLine();

Expected behavior

I would expect not to see that warning in this case, either because the analyzer can track the variable assignment (this is a relatively simple pattern of a fully awaited task that definitely assigns to the variable), or because it's too complex and can't tell for sure.

Actual behavior

Instead, the analyzer seems confident that the assignment never takes place.

item.title: Issue: Unreachable null check after call to GetSpecialType - @Youssef1313
item.description:
https://github.com/dotnet/roslyn-analyzers/blob/cab4a924d6e23560508eabdd802455d0eae18752/src/NetAnalyzers/Core/Microsoft.NetCore.Analyzers/Runtime/SpecifyStringComparison.cs#L61

It looks like stringType can never be null here. It can be an error type.

item.title: Issue: False positive from RS1024 (Compare symbols correctly) when initializing a HashSet<ITypeSymbol> - @AArnott
item.description:
Analyzer

Diagnostic ID: RS1024: Compare symbols correctly

Analyzer source

Package: Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Workspaces Version: 3.10.0

Describe the bug

This line produces the RS1024 diagnostic, even though we do specify to use the SymbolEqualityComparer:

private readonly HashSet<ITypeSymbol> alreadyCollected = new(SymbolEqualityComparer.Default);

item.title: Issue: CA1508 object?.Content == null is always 'false', when checking two different objects from same async function. - @nicro950
item.description:
Analyzer

Diagnostic ID: CA1508: Avoid dead conditional code

Analyzer source

SDK: Built-in CA analyzers in .NET 5 SDK or later

Version: SDK 6.0.0-preview.6.21353.1

Describe the bug

When using the following code, the analyzer complains that the obj2?.Content == null is always false, even if we set it to null. It looks like the analyzer thinks that obj == obj2 since it is returned by the same async function. If the async is removed, then the warning disappears.

        static async Task Main(string[] args)
        {
            var obj = await GetSomeObject("something");
            if (obj?.Content == null)
                return;

            var obj2 = await GetSomeObject(null);
            if (obj2?.Content == null) // The warning is on this line saying obj2.Content == null is always false
                return;
        }

        static Task<SomeObject> GetSomeObject(string? content)
        {
            return Task.FromResult(new SomeObject() { Content = content });
        }
        public class SomeObject
        {
            public string? Content { get; set; }
        }

Steps To Reproduce

  1. Create new project, this was created with .net 5, but with dotnet 6 preview 6 installed
  2. Enable project analyzers in csproj with:
    <EnableNETAnalyzers>true</EnableNETAnalyzers>
    <AnalysisMode>AllEnabledByDefault</AnalysisMode>
  1. Paste the code into the console app, and the code should warn about == null is always false.

Expected behavior

Not reporting warning

Actual behavior

False positive warning

item.title: Issue: Update Wiki for several issues - @Youssef1313
item.description:
https://github.com/dotnet/roslyn-analyzers/wiki

  • Install Instructions

    • [ ] Shouldn't mention VS Extension.
    • [ ] Should mention the SDK, and possibly link to https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/fundamentals/code-analysis/overview#code-quality-analysis

  • Apply a Code Fix for a Rule

    • [x] There is a dead image link due to the movement from master to main. This link should be fixed.
    • [ ] There is a statement saying not all 100 code analysis rules in this extension have fixes associated with them., if the number of rules is now much greater than 100, this should be fixed either by removing the number at all, or fix the number.

  • Configure a Rule

    • [x] Dead image
    • [ ] It mentions ruleset, this should be updated for globalconfig and/or editorconfig

  • FAQ

    • [ ] Shouldn't mention rulesets, or mention them as obsolete/deprecated.
    • [ ] A question saying Does this work with EditorConfig? is answered by "No". This feels outdated.
    • [ ] Links to NuGet package are for the old FxCopAnalyzers, not NetAnalyzers.

item.title: Issue: CA1419 shouldn't fire for abstract SafeHandle-derived types - @stephentoub
item.description:
Analyzer

Diagnostic ID: CA1419: Provide a public parameterless constructor for concrete types derived from 'System.Runtime.InteropServices.SafeHandle

Analyzer source

SDK: Built-in CA analyzers in .NET 6 SDK or later

Describe the bug

The analyzer is firing for abstract SafeHandle-derived types. They're not directly constructable and don't need a "public" parameterless ctor.

Steps To Reproduce

Create an abstract type derived from SafeHandle without a public parameterless ctor.

Expected behavior

No warnings.

Actual behavior

CA1419 fires.

cc: @jkoritzinsky

item.title: Issue: CA1419 is firing for cases that aren't related to P/Invokes - @stephentoub
item.description:
Analyzer

Diagnostic ID: CA1419: Provide a public parameterless constructor for concrete types derived from 'System.Runtime.InteropServices.SafeHandle

Analyzer source

SDK: Built-in CA analyzers in .NET 6 SDK or later

Describe the bug

The purpose of CA1419 is to help make SafeHandles friendly for a source generator, which requires access to the ctor to avoid using reflection as the runtime would. If there's no ctor the runtime could use for marshaling, the rule shouldn't fire. It should only fire in cases where there's a ctor that the runtime could have used but where that ctor won't be visible for a source generator.

Steps To Reproduce

Example: https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/blob/004feb873271a7e5d9862b4b0dd1edb8e1db9543/src/libraries/System.Private.Runtime.InteropServices.JavaScript/src/System/Runtime/InteropServices/JavaScript/DataView.cs#L12

Expected behavior

No warning

Actual behavior

CA1419 fires.

cc: @jkoritzinsky

item.title: Issue: CA1419 is too restrictive on the ctor required - @stephentoub
item.description:
Analyzer

Diagnostic ID: CA1419: Provide a public parameterless constructor for concrete types derived from 'System.Runtime.InteropServices.SafeHandle

Analyzer source

SDK: Built-in CA analyzers in .NET 6 SDK or later

Describe the bug

CA1419 is requiring that SafeHandles have a public parameterless ctor. This is too restrictive. Most custom SafeHandles are themselves internal to a project, so requiring it have a public ctor is prohibitive; to satisfy the purpose of the rule (to be usable with a source generator that needs to be able to construct the SafeHandle), the ctor need only be as visible as the type itself, e.g. internal if internal, etc.

Steps To Reproduce

Derive an internal type from SafeHandle and add an internal parameterless ctor.

Expected behavior

No warning.

Actual behavior

CA1419 fires.

cc: @jkoritzinsky

item.title: Issue: Fix TODOs in DetectPreviewAnalyzer.cs - @pgovind
item.description: Fix the TODOs in the code and the action items called out in https://github.com/dotnet/roslyn-analyzers/pull/5155's PR description

item.title: Issue: Asterisk should be escaped in generated markdown files - @Youssef1313
item.description:
This is a bug in markdown generation. The asterisks should be escaped. I'll file a follow-up issue on that.

Originally posted by @Youssef1313 in https://github.com/dotnet/roslyn-analyzers/pull/5210#r665961235

item.title: Issue: CA2208 should not report a violation when `paramName` not specified in a property - @xtqqczze
item.description:
Analyzer

Diagnostic ID: CA2208: Instantiate argument exceptions correctly

Describe the improvement

Specifying value as a paramName for ArgumentException in a property does not add any value over what the stack trace provides, therefore a violation should not be reported.

Example

Existing violation

internal string Property
{
    set
    {
        _backing = value ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(); // CA2208 violation
    }
}

Creates the message:

Value cannot be null.

Fix of violation

internal string Property
{
    set
    {
        _backing = value ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(value));
    }
}

Creates the message:

Value cannot be null.
Parameter name: value

Additional context

The book, Framework Design Guidelines (2008) from Addison-Wesley Microsoft Technology Series does recommend using the implicit value parameter of property setters for paramName but it is not explained why this is worthwhile.

See also

https://github.com/dotnet/roslyn-analyzers/issues/1561

item.title: Issue: CA1416 does not recognise built-in guards for macOS-specific types - @yaakov-h
item.description:
Analyzer

Diagnostic ID: CA1416: Validate platform compatibility

Analyzer source

SDK: Built-in CA analyzers in .NET 5 SDK or later

Version: SDK 6.0.100-preview.5.21302.13 (included in VS2022 Preview 1)

Describe the bug

CA1416 triggers when I try to use a type annotated as supporting "osx" or "macos", when I guard the calls with RuntimeInformation.IsOSPlatform(OSPlatforms.OSX).

Steps To Reproduce

Compile the following code example:

using System.Runtime.InteropServices;
using System.Runtime.Versioning;

namespace DemoSupportedPlatformLib
{
    public abstract class PlatformSpecificFunction
    {
        public static PlatformSpecificFunction GetProvider()
        {
            if (RuntimeInformation.IsOSPlatform(OSPlatform.Windows))
            {
                return new WindowsSpecificFunction();
            }
            else if (RuntimeInformation.IsOSPlatform(OSPlatform.OSX))
            {
                return new OSXSpecificFunction();
            }
            else if (RuntimeInformation.IsOSPlatform(OSPlatform.Linux))
            {
                return new LinuxSpecificFunction();
            }
            else
            {
                return new DefaultFunction();
            }
        }
    }

    [SupportedOSPlatform("windows")]
    class WindowsSpecificFunction : PlatformSpecificFunction
    {
    }

    [SupportedOSPlatform("macos")]
    class OSXSpecificFunction : PlatformSpecificFunction
    {
    }

    [SupportedOSPlatform("linux")]
    class LinuxSpecificFunction : PlatformSpecificFunction
    {
    }

    class DefaultFunction : PlatformSpecificFunction
    {
    }
}

Expected behavior

No build warnings or errors.

Actual behavior

warning CA1416: This call site is reachable on: 'OSX'. 'OSXSpecificFunction' is only supported on: 'macOS/OSX'.

item.title: Issue: Code fix for CA1820 could be improved for `string.Equals` - @xtqqczze
item.description:
Analyzer

Diagnostic ID: CA1820

Describe the improvement

In the case of an empty string using str.Equals(string.Empty), it can be assumed that str should not be null, as this would have resulted in a NullReferenceException.

Current code fix

string.IsNullOrEmpty(str)

Expected code fix

str.Length == 0

Additional context

This approach was suggested in #4537, however was not implemented.

item.title: Issue: Are .analyzerconfig supported targeting .netstandard 2.1? - @JuanZamudioGBM
item.description:
Analyzer

Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.NetAnalyzers

SDK: 3.1.410

Version: 5.0.3 (Latest)

Describe the bug

We have a library that supports .net5 and .netstandard2.1, "dotnet build" with the SDK 5.0.301 runs correctly, but using the SDK 3.1.410 the rules in analyzerconfig are ignored

Expected behavior

configuration in analyzerconfig is respected

Actual behavior

configuration in analyzerconfig is ignored

item.title: Issue: Analyzer complaining about not disposing object (False positives) - @roywill
item.description:
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.NetAnalyzers" Version="5.0.3">
      <PrivateAssets>all</PrivateAssets>
      <IncludeAssets>runtime; build; native; contentfiles; analyzers; buildtransitive</IncludeAssets>
    </PackageReference>

Describe the bug

var item = new ItemData(scannedFileName: filePath); return parseChannel.Writer.WriteAsync(item);

The class ItemData is indeed IDisposable and WriteAsync can throw, so yep I would an IDisposable error, which there is.

Changing to:

So, to address the issue I change that to this.

var item = new ItemData(scannedFileName: filePath); try { var parseTask = parseChannel.Writer.WriteAsync(item); item = null; return parseTask; } finally { item?.Dispose(); }

Only results in:

Severity | Code | Description | Project | File | Line | Suppression State | Tool -- | -- | -- | -- | -- | -- | -- | -- Warning | CA1508 | 'item' is always 'null'. Remove or refactor the condition(s) to avoid dead code. | ItemCheck | ... | 636 | Active | Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.NetAnalyzers

Severity | Code | Description | Project | File | Line | Suppression State | Tool -- | -- | -- | -- | -- | -- | -- | -- Warning | CA2000 | Call System.IDisposable.Dispose on object created by 'new ItemData(scannedFileName: filePath)' before all references to it are out of scope | ItemCheck | ... | 627 | Active | Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.NetAnalyzers

item.title: Issue: NetAnalyzers does not show errors in Visual studio 16.10.3 during build - @JuanZamudioGBM
item.description:
Analyzer

Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.NetAnalyzers,

SDK: 5.0.301

Version: 5.0.3 (Latest)

Describe the bug

I moved away from FxCopAnalyzers to NetAnalyzers, and some errors are not showing in Visual Studio while doing a rebuild, I have to run dotnet build to see the errors, after seeing the errors in the CLI if I open the class VS now shows the errors.

Steps To Reproduce

I have this in a unit test

SymmetricType symmetricType = SymmetricType.TripleDes;
using Symmetric target = new Symmetric(symmetricType);
...
target.Mode = CipherMode.ECB;
....

during the compilation I din not get any error, after runing dotnet build and opening the class the following error appears in the error list in VS Error CA5358 Review the usage of cipher mode 'ECB' with cryptography experts.

Expected behavior

Show the errors on visual studio

Actual behavior

Solution build successfully in Visual studio but fails with the CLI on Windows 10 with the SDK 5.0.301, fails in AWS with the same SDK, but runs fine in azure using the agent windows-2019.

item.title: Issue: CA1840 error link broken - @allisonchou
item.description: CA1840 links to this webpage, which is broken, so I cannot find more information out about the diagnostic. The screenshot below is from working in the aspnetcore-tooling repository in VS2022.

item.title: Issue: "configure code analysis" leads to deprecated property page - @vsfeedback
item.description:
This issue has been moved from a ticket on Developer Community.

[severity:I'm unable to use this version] This is not a duplicate of https://developercommunity.visualstudio.com/t/code-analysis-settings-doesnt-load/317861

When selecting "Analyze > Configure Code Analysis > For Solution" it opens a deprecated property page.

Additionally, the deprecated property page has a link to a Code analysis FAQ which was written for .NET despite all projects in this solution being C++

Original Comments

Feedback Bot on 3/1/2021, 10:52 PM:

We have directed your feedback to the appropriate engineering team for further evaluation. The team will review the feedback and notify you about the next steps.

Feedback Bot on 3/3/2021, 08:57 AM:

Thank you for sharing your feedback! Our teams prioritize action on product issues with broad customer impact. See details at: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/visualstudio/ide/report-a-problem?view=vs-2019#faq. In case you need answers to common questions or need assisted support, be sure to use https://visualstudio.microsoft.com/vs/support/. We’ll keep you posted on any updates to this feedback.

Feedback Bot on 5/31/2021, 08:23 PM:

I detected that this issue hasn’t received a lot of activity, votes, or comments in the past 90 days. Based on this, the issues severity and affected area, it’s my experience that this issue is unlikely to get fixed. To improve the situation, consider following best practices for quality problem reports, and giving us more details on how this issue is impacting you.

Original Solutions

(no solutions)

item.title: Issue: New rule for private field names requiring an underscore - @DrGriff
item.description:
Describe the problem you are trying to solve

From C# Coding Style it states that:

  1. We use _camelCase for internal and private fields and use readonly where possible. Prefix internal and private instance fields with _, static fields with s_ and thread static fields with t_. When used on static fields, readonly should come after static (e.g. static readonly not readonly static). Public fields should be used sparingly and should use PascalCasing with no prefix when used.

I'm not seeing a rule that requires private fields to be prefixed with an underscore.

Describe suggestions on how to achieve the rule

So the rule should find all private/internal fields that don't start with an underscore (+ camelCased maybe too?) name and flag them. Ideally have a helper that will fix this instance, all instances in this document/project/solution.

There is an existing rule https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/fundamentals/code-analysis/quality-rules/ca1707 which might conflict - not sure.

Additional context

item.title: Issue: Configure severity of security related rules by category - @yifanbian-msft
item.description: Hi, we would like to enable all security related rules in our projects, but I found that dotnet_analyzer_diagnostic.category-Security.severity = error doesn't work. After digging into .NET Analyzer source code, I found that RuleLevel.Disabled documentation states that these rules can't be enabled using the category based bulk configuration. Could you help me on understanding this design, or make it bulk-configurable? Thanks.

item.title: Issue: Analyzer Configuration for CA1051,CA1826 not available in .NET5.0 - @wzchua
item.description:
Analyzer

Diagnostic ID: CA1051,CA1826, maybe some others

Analyzer source

SDK: Built-in CA analyzers in .NET 5 SDK or later

Version: SDK 5.0.301

Describe the bug

Analyzer Configuration is not taken into account

Steps To Reproduce

  1. create a new console project.
  2. Add .editorconfig
root = true

[*.cs]
dotnet_diagnostic.CA1051.severity = warning
dotnet_code_quality.exclude_structs = true
  1. Add a struct with a public field to the project
public readonly struct S
{
    public readonly int Field;
}
  1. dotnet build

Expected behavior

No warnings

Actual behavior

Warning CA1051

item.title: Issue: new compiler message on duplicate XML docs instead of `<inheritdoc/>` - @ZacharyPatten
item.description:
B contains copy-pasted <summary>hello world</summary> when <inheritdoc/> would prevent duplication.

Note: this could be a pain point as not all tools support inheritdoc, but in those cases I would expect people to know the limitations of the tools they are using and suppress this message

/// <summary>hello world</summary>
public class A { }

/// <summary>hello world</summary>
public class B : A { }

item.title: Issue: new compiler message on unnecessary `cref` in an `inheritdoc` - @ZacharyPatten
item.description:
Example 1

The <inheritdoc cref="A"/> below could be <inheritdoc/>.

/// <summary>hello world</summary>
public class A { }

/// <inheritdoc cref="A"/>
public class B : A { }

Example 2

The <inheritdoc cref="A"/> below could be <inheritdoc/>.

/// <summary>hello world</summary>
public class A { }

/// <inheritdoc/>
public class B : A { }

/// <inheritdoc cref="A"/>
public class C : B { }

Example 3

The <inheritdoc cref="A"/> and <inheritdoc cref="B"/> below could be replaced with a single <inheritdoc/>.

Note: if the order of the inheritdocs on C were reversed and B overrides any of As XML docs, then the crefs are required and this message should not appear.

/// <summary>hello world</summary>
public class A { }

/// <inheritdoc/>
public class B : A { }

/// <inheritdoc cref="B"/>
/// <inheritdoc cref="A"/>
public class C : B { }

item.title: Issue: new compiler message on unnecessary `inheritdoc`s - @ZacharyPatten
item.description:
Example 1

The <inheritdoc /> on B is unnecessary because all the XML from A is overriden.

/// <summary>hello world</summary>
public class A { }

/// <summary>goodbye world</summary>
/// <inheritdoc />
public class B : A { }

Example 2

The duplicate <inheritdoc />s on B could be a single <inheritdoc />.

/// <summary>hello world</summary>
public class A { }

/// <inheritdoc />
/// <inheritdoc />
public class B : A { }

Example 3

The duplicate <inheritdoc cref="A"/>s on B could be a single <inheritdoc cref="A"/>.

/// <summary>hello world</summary>
public class A { }

/// <inheritdoc cref="A"/>
/// <inheritdoc cref="A"/>
public class B { }

Example 4

The XML nodes <inheritdoc cref="A"/> and <inheritdoc cref="B"/> on C could be a single <inheritdoc cref="A"/> because all of the XML from <inheritdoc cref="B"/> is being overriden by the <inheritdoc cref="A"/>.

/// <summary>hello world</summary>
public class A { }

/// <summary>goodbye world</summary>
public class B { }

/// <inheritdoc cref="A"/>
/// <inheritdoc cref="B"/>
public class C { }

Example 5

B has an inheritdoc+cref targetting A, but A has no XML docs.

public class A { }

/// <inheritdoc cref="A"/>
public class B { }

Example 6

A has a self referencing inheritdoc.

Note: this may warrant a compiler warning (rather than a compiler message) as it is likely a typo

/// <inheritdoc cref="A"/>
public class A { }

Example 7

A.Method has an inheritdoc but there is no base class or interface.

Note: this is the same as SA1648. It should be pulled into roslyn-analyzers.

public class A
{
	/// <inheritdoc />
	public void Method() { }
}

Example 8

B has a <remarks> nested inheritdoc+cref targetting A, which means that it is attempting to inherit the <remarks> of A, but A has no <remarks> to be inherited. A only has a <summary>, no <remarks>.

/// <summary>hello world</summary>
public class A { }

/// <remarks><inheritdoc cref="A"/></remarks>
public class B { }

item.title: Issue: false positive for CA1801 for "args" in top level statements - @adamralph
item.description:
Analyzer

Diagnostic ID: CA1801: Review unused parameters

Analyzer source

SDK: Built-in CA analyzers in .NET 5 SDK or later

Version: SDK 5.0.302

Note: I was informed by @jmarolf that SDK 5.0.301 should have the bug fix for https://github.com/dotnet/roslyn-analyzers/pull/4066 so I did not investigate whether latest NuGet package has this bug.

Describe the bug

The diagnostic is reported for the implicit args parameter for top level statements.

Steps To Reproduce

Provide the steps to reproduce the behavior:

  1. Clone https://github.com/adamralph/dotnet-roslyn-analyzers-4066
  2. Build the solution

Expected behavior

CA1801 is not reported.

Actual behavior

CA1801 is reported.

Additional context

The problem is shown here when using SDK 5.0.302.

The problem is shown here when using SDK 5.0.301.

item.title: Issue: CA1704 needs to be able to turn off unmeaningful identifier diagnostic for single letter symbol name - @kemats
item.description:
Analyzer

Diagnostic ID: CA1704

Describe the improvement

CA1704 in Text.Analyzers always reports unmeaningful identifier for single letter symbol name even when the single letter is on custom dictionary as a recognized word. This can cause producing many CA1704 because there are commonly used single letter symbol names such as 'EventArgs e'.

Describe suggestions on how to achieve the rule

Ignore a single letter symbol names from check for unmeaningful identifier when the single letter is on custom dictionary as a recognized word. I believe this is how CA1704 in Legacy FxCop treats single letter symbol names.

Legacy FxCop treats the case where the type of identifier named 'e' is derived from System.EventArgs as a special case, and does not report unmeaningful identifier. It is not easy for Roslyn Analyzers to treat this special case and personally I don't think it's necessary to do that, if CA1704 is able to turn off unmeaningful identifier diagnostic for single letter symbol name.

Additional context

Attached is a sample to compare how CA1704 handles single letter symbol names between Text.Analyzers and Legacy FxCop. SingleLetterCheckRepro.zip

item.title: Issue: Multiple issues with Attribute as banned symbol - @MaceWindu
item.description:
Analyzer

Diagnostic ID: RS0030

Describe the improvement

I have following scenario:

  • there is an attribute that is not allowed to use directly (attribute type banned by rule)
  • instead of this attribute we have new attribute type, derived from banned one

There are two issues with banned api analyzer I see:

  1. Deriving from banned type doesn't rise RS0030 (I would expect it to produce error that I need to supporess in this case)
// no RS0030 here
public class MyAttribute : BannedAttribute
{
}
  1. access to banned type properties on derived type rise RS0030
// RS0030 generated here for Description property, defined in BannedAttribute
// but I didn't banned property explicitly and we deal with non-banned MyAttribute type here
[My(Description = "this is method")]
publict void Method() {}

Workaround:

public class MyAttribute : BannedAttribute
{
        public new string? Description
        {
#pragma warning disable RS0030 // Do not used banned APIs
            get => base.Description;
            set => base.Description = value;
#pragma warning restore RS0030 // Do not used banned APIs
        }
}
  1. I'm not native speaker but Do not used banned APIs sounds wrong to me
  2. use of banned attribute with named properties generate N+1 errors
// RS0030 for Banned
// RS0030 for Banned.Prop1
// RS0030 for Banned.Prop2
[Banned(Prop1 = 1, Prop2 = 2)]

Sorry for multiple issues in single report. If needed I can split it into separate issues

item.title: Issue: dotnet build --no-resotre CSC : error CS8034 - @bluekms
item.description:
Analyzer

Diagnostic ID: [CS8034]

Analyzer source

SDK: Built-in CA analyzers in .NET 5 SDK or later Version: SDK 5.0.x NuGet Package: Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.NetAnalyzers Version: 5.0.3 (Latest)

Describe the bug

Solution configuration

  1. My Lib Project
  2. LibTester (for TDD) (ref 1 project)
  3. Playground Project (ref 1, 2 project)

dotnet build fail

Run dotnet build --no-restore

Build FAILED.

CSC : error CS8034: Unable to load Analyzer assembly /home/runner/.nuget/packages/microsoft.codeanalysis.netanalyzers/5.0.3/analyzers/dotnet/cs/Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.NetAnalyzers.dll : Assembly with same name is already loaded [/home/runner/work/Utility/Utility/UtilityLib/UtilityLib.csproj] CSC : error CS8034: Unable to load Analyzer assembly /home/runner/.nuget/packages/microsoft.codeanalysis.netanalyzers/5.0.3/analyzers/dotnet/cs/Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.NetAnalyzers.dll : Assembly with same name is already loaded [/home/runner/work/Utility/Utility/UtilityLib/UtilityLib.csproj] 0 Warning(s) 2 Error(s)

but release build success

dotnet build --no-restore --configuration Release

Success.

I can't understand. In all circumstances, we want to avoid errors and warnings. I need help. Thank you.

Steps To Reproduce

  1. Create solution
  2. Create lib project
  3. Create Test project (Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk) (ref lib project)
  4. Create console project (ref lib project and tdd project)
  5. dotnet restore
  6. Run dotnet build --no-restore

Expected behavior

Build Success

Actual behavior

Build FAILED. CS8034

item.title: Issue: CA1822 (Mark members as static) codefix doesn't add static to partial definitions - @Youssef1313
item.description: See tests added in https://github.com/dotnet/roslyn-analyzers/pull/5172.

item.title: Issue: Any reason not to have DiagnosticDescriptor readonly - @Evangelink
item.description:
Hi there,

I was browsing the code base and realized that the DiagnosticDescriptor declared are internal static but not readonly while they are not expected to change. Is there a technical (perf?) reason to do so?

item.title: Issue: partial method incorrectly triggers CA1822 - @dlech
item.description:
Version Used: .NET 6 preview 5

Steps to Reproduce:

  1. Install .NET 6 preview 5 + VS Code + ms-dotnettools.csharp extension.
  2. git clone https://github.com/dlech/CA1822
  3. code CA1822
  4. Open Class1.cs in the project.
  5. Look for squiggles.

Expected Behavior:

The code should not trigger any code analyzers.

Actual Behavior:

As indicated by the comment in the code, it triggers CA1822 on the partial declaration. This incorrectly suggests that the method does not use instance data and could be made static.

item.title: Issue: CA1508: false positive when when combining null-coalesce with using statement - @innix
item.description:
Analyzer

Diagnostic ID: CA1508: Avoid dead conditional code

Analyzer source

SDK: Built-in CA analyzers in .NET 5 SDK or later

Version: SDK 5.0.301

Describe the bug

I enabled all the analyzers on 1 of my projects and noticed this false positive:

public static void Test(int? n)
{
    var ms = new MemoryStream();

    _ = n ?? 300;

    using (ms)
    {
        // ...
    }
}

Steps To Reproduce

Compile the above program with the following properties set in your .csproj file:

<PropertyGroup>
  <EnableNETAnalyzers>true</EnableNETAnalyzers>
  <AnalysisMode>AllEnabledByDefault</AnalysisMode>
</PropertyGroup>

Expected behavior

using (ms) should not be flagged as dead code. I'm not even performing a null check so I assume it's analyzing some compiler-generated code.

Actual behavior

It gets flagged.

Additional context

  • The false positive is not triggered by the typical ways the using statement is written:

    
// Fine.
using (var ms = new MemoryStream())
{
    // ...
}

// Also fine.
using var ms = new MemoryStream();
// ...

  • You can move the null-coalesce line anywhere in the method and it still triggers the false positive on the using statement line.
